content.content:
# CSS Masonry Layout Property

Based on the MDN documentation, masonry layout is enabled using:

```css
display: grid-lanes;
```

or

```css
display: inline-grid-lanes;
```

## Specification Details

- **Specification Level**: Level 3 of the CSS Grid Layout specification
- **Official Spec**: [CSS Grid Layout Module Level 3 - Grid-lanes model](https://drafts.csswg.org/css-grid-3/#grid-lanes-model)

## How It Works

When using `display: grid-lanes`, you pair it with standard grid properties like `grid-template-columns`:

```css
.grid {
  display: grid-lanes;
  gap: 10px;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(120px, 1fr));
}
```

**Key behavior**: One axis uses a strict grid layout (typically columns), while the other axis uses a **stacking/masonry layout**. Items fill into the column with the most available space, creating a tightly packed layout without strict row constraints.

## Important Notes

- ⚠️ **Experimental**: This is an experimental technology with **limited browser support**
- **Fallback**: In unsupported browsers, regular grid auto-placement is used instead
- The property is **not yet Baseline** - it doesn't work in all widely-used browsers
